task_completion.description: The agent successfully located and opened the demographic record for Emily Williams.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen in Oscar EMR showing the patient name 'Emily Williams' in the demographic search results, master record, or edit view.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the new address, city, province, and postal code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The demographic edit form shows '200 Bay Street, Suite 1500' in the address field, 'Toronto' in the city field, 'ON' in the province field, and 'M5J 2J2' in the postal code field.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the new home phone number.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The demographic edit form shows '647-555-0187' in the Home Phone input field.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the new email address.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The demographic edit form shows 'emily.williams.new@gmail.com' in the Email input field.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the updated demographic record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks an 'Update', 'Save', or 'Update Record' button, or the final screenshot shows the patient's summary page with the newly updated information reflecting the saved state.

integrity.description: The agent used the Oscar EMR web interface in a browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the Oscar EMR URL (e.g., localhost:8080/oscar/...) and the standard Oscar EMR UI is visible, rather than a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use the terminal to update the database directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show SQL commands, curl requests, or scripts being executed to update patient demographics.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's Developer Tools (Elements tab, Console) are not open and being used to modify the page DOM to display the expected values.
